items.title: Transformer fit vs transform
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  trainRaw["training raw texts"] --> fitStep["fit: learn vocabulary / idf"]
  fitStep --> state["frozen extractor state"]
  trainRaw --> transformTrain["transform → X_train"]
  state --> transformTrain
  testRaw["test raw texts"] --> transformTest["transform → X_test"]
  state --> transformTest
items.caption: fit learns parameters once from training data; transform applies them to any split without refitting.

items.title: Text to sparse feature matrix
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  doc["document string"] --> token["tokenize + optional ngrams"]
  token --> vocab["map token → column index"]
  vocab --> sparse["sparse row vector"]
  sparse --> matrix["document-term matrix X"]
items.caption: Vectorizers compile strings into rows of a sparse matrix suitable for classical ML estimators.

items.title: Pipeline with feature extractor
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  raw["raw inputs"] --> ext["Transformer: vectorizer"]
  ext --> Xmat["numeric X"]
  Xmat --> model["classifier / regressor"]
  model --> pred["predictions"]
items.caption: In a Pipeline, fit propagates through extractor then model; predict runs transform then predict on the estimator.

items.title: Count vs TF–IDF vs hashing
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  text["corpus of texts"] --> count["CountVectorizer: integer counts"]
  text --> tfidf["TfidfVectorizer: weighted counts"]
  text --> hash["HashingVectorizer: fixed hash buckets"]
  count --> nb["Multinomial NB / count models"]
  tfidf --> linear["LinearSVC / LogisticRegression"]
  hash --> stream["large-scale / streaming"]
items.caption: Choose vectorizer by model expectations, interpretability needs, and vocabulary size constraints.
